title: Fluid treatment apparatus and methods
abstract: An apparatus comprises a vessel for containing a fluid to be treated, a variable DC voltage source, and two or more electrodes disposed such that they contact the fluid in the vessel and are electrically connected to the DC voltage source, and the fluid is electrically isolated from ground except via an electrode. The DC voltage source provides DC voltage that varies relative to ground. The apparatus may be used for adjusting parameters of a fluid, such as adjusting pH and adjusting electrode potential by adding or removing electrons, without using additional chemicals or agents. The apparatus and methods may be used to promote and control chemical reactions, including reactions for the destruction of toxic compounds and drugs, to promote sedimentation of particulate matter and to precipitate heavy metals and selected compounds, and to kill microorganisms and destroy biological compounds.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An apparatus, comprising: a vessel for containing a fluid to be treated; a variable DC voltage source; two or more electrodes disposed such that they contact the fluid in the vessel and are electrically connected to the variable DC voltage source; wherein the fluid is electrically isolated from ground except via an electrode; wherein the variable DC voltage source provides an output voltage that varies relative to ground. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the two or more electrodes are electrically connected to the variable DC voltage source such that one or more electrode is always a cathode and one or more electrode is always an anode. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the two or more electrodes are electrically connected to the variable DC voltage source such that one or more first electrode is alternately a cathode for a first period of time and then an anode for a second period of time, and, simultaneously, one or more second electrode is alternately an anode for the first period of time and then a cathode for the second period of time. 4. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein at least one of an amplitude and a frequency of the variable DC voltage source is adjustable. 5. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 4, further comprising an electrical connection to ground via one or more anode electrode. 6. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 5, further comprising an actuator that causes the fluid to pass between the two or more electrodes. 7. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the variable DC voltage has a waveform selected from a half sine wave, a sine wave, a square wave, a triangular wave, and a saw tooth wave. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the waveform has a positive voltage offset relative to ground greater than or equal to one-half of a waveform peak-to-peak voltage and less than the waveform peak-to-peak voltage. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the variable DC voltage varies within a range of about 1 volt and about 500 volts. 10. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the voltage offset provides a minimum waveform voltage of negative 4.5 volts relative to ground. 11. The apparatus of any one of claims 2 to 10, wherein a voltage applied to the cathode electrode is between negative 4.5 volts relative to ground and positive 500 volts relative to ground. 12. The apparatus of any one of claims 2 to 10, wherein a voltage applied to the anode electrode is as low as negative 4.5 volts relative to ground. 13. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein a frequency range of the variable DC voltage is from 1 Hz to 300 MHz. 14. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the apparatus is used with an aqueous fluid to lower a pH of the fluid to a minimum of zero or less or to raise the pH to 14 or greater without the use of an additional chemical or agent. 15. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the apparatus is used to increase electrode potential of the fluid to a more positive value or to lower the electrode potential of the fluid to a more negative value without the use of an additional chemical or agent. 16. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the apparatus is used to add or remove electrons from the fluid. 17. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the apparatus is used to increase conductivity of the fluid without the use of an additional chemical agent or an electrolyte. 18. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the apparatus is used to precipitate at least one metal from the fluid. 19. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the apparatus is used to destroy or reduce or eliminate at least one biological contaminant in the fluid, or to destroy or reduce or eliminate toxicity of at least one chemical compound in the fluid. 20. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the apparatus is used to kill, destroy, or render unviable at least one microorganism in the fluid. 21. A method for adjusting electrode potential of a fluid, comprising: containing the fluid within a vessel; 

 exposing the fluid to two or more electrodes electrically connected to a variable DC voltage source, wherein the fluid is electrically isolated from ground; and applying a variable DC voltage that varies relative to ground to the two or more electrodes; wherein the electrode potential of the fluid is adjusted without use of an additional chemical or agent. 22. The method of claim 21 , wherein the two or more electrodes are electrically connected to the variable DC voltage source such that one or more electrode is always a cathode and one or more electrode is always an anode. 23. The method of claim 21, wherein the two or more electrodes are electrically connected to the variable DC voltage source such that one or more first electrode is alternately a cathode for a first period of time and then an anode for a second period of time, and, simultaneously, one or more second electrode is alternately an anode for the first period of time and then a cathode for the second period of time. 24. The method of claim 21 , further comprising adjusting pH of the fluid; comprising providing an electrical connection to ground via the one or more anode electrode. 25. The method of any one of claims 21 to 24, further comprising providing an actuator that causes the fluid to pass between the two or more electrodes. 26. The method of any one of claims 21 to 25, wherein the output voltage that varies relative to ground has a waveform selected from a half sine wave, a sine wave, a square wave, a triangle wave and a saw tooth wave. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the waveform has a positive voltage offset relative to ground greater than or equal to one-half of a waveform peak-to-peak voltage and less than the waveform peak-to-peak voltage. 28. The method of claim 21 , wherein the variable DC voltage varies within a range of about 1 volt and about 500 volts. 29. The method of claim 27, wherein the positive voltage offset provides a minimum waveform voltage of negative 4.5 volts relative to ground. 30. The method of any one of claims 22 to 29, wherein a voltage applied to the cathode electrode is between negative 4.5 volts relative to ground and positive 500 volts relative to ground. 31. The method of any one of claims 22 to 28, wherein a voltage applied to the anode electrode is as low as negative 4.5 volts relative to ground. 32. The method of any one of claims 21 to 31, wherein a frequency range of the output voltage is from 1 Hz to 300 MHz. 33. The method any one of claims 21 to 32, wherein the method lowers the pH of an aqueous fluid to a minimum of zero or raises the pH of an aqueous fluid to 14 or greater without the use of an additional chemical or agent. 34. The method any one of claims 21 to 32, wherein the method increases electrode potential of the fluid to a more positive value or lowers the electrode potential of the fluid to a more negative value without the use of an additional chemical or agent. 35. The method of any one of claims 21 to 32, wherein the method adds or removes electrons from the fluid. 36. The method of any one of claims 21 to 32, wherein the method increases conductivity of the fluid without the use of an additional chemical agent or an electrolyte. 37. The method of any one of claims 21 to 32, comprising precipitating at least one metal from the fluid. 38. The method of any one of claims 21 to 32, comprising destroying, reducing, or eliminating at least one biological contaminant in the fluid, or destroying, reducing, or eliminating toxicity of at least one chemical compound in the fluid. 39. The method of any one of claims 21 to 32, comprising killing, destroying, or rendering unviable at least one microorganism in the fluid.
